AMAT.headline: Top-line decelerating as China share erodes; memory/HBM mix expanding within the SemiSys segment.

shifts.trend: FY25 revenue +4% YoY → Q1 FY26 -2% YoY
shifts.note: Weakness concentrated in non-leading-edge logic; HBM-driven memory is a partial offset

shifts.trend: China 37% of SemiSys in FY24 → 30% in FY25 → further contraction in Q1 FY26; Taiwan jumped to 24%
shifts.note: Geographic mix shifting away from China toward advanced Taiwan/Korea logic

shifts.trend: DRAM share of SemiSys revenue 27% (FY25 avg) → 34% (Q1 FY26) as HBM capex from MU/SK accelerates
shifts.note: Memory-capex tail is extending AMAT's growth window beyond weak logic cycle

shifts.trend: Gross margin 48.7% (FY25) → stable at similar run-rate in Q1 FY26
shifts.note: Customer concentration unchanged: two customers at 19%/15%

AMAT.verdict: Mix shift toward memory/HBM is bullish for AMAT's long-cycle revenue but doesn't offset the China headcount and the logic-node softness; top-line deceleration is the primary risk while the HBM cycle elongates.

AMD.headline: DC segment re-accelerating to +57% YoY in Q1 FY26; gross margin expanding 3pts on richer mix; China optionality partially restored.

shifts.trend: Data Center YoY: +32% (FY25) → +57% (Q1 FY26) as MI350 wins hyperscaler slots alongside EPYC
shifts.note: MI450 OpenAI deal ramp not yet fully in revenue

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP gross margin ~51% (FY25 avg) → 53% (Q1 FY26) on DC mix enrichment
shifts.note: 3pt expansion in a single quarter — fastest in recent history

shifts.trend: MI325X export-control restriction partially lifted with 25% US re-import tariff; no China revenue in FY25 → partial restoration in Q1 FY26
shifts.note: Dollar impact not yet material but removes a bearish overhang

shifts.trend: DC mix growing as % of total; EPYC server share gains combined with Instinct GPU ramp driving mix enrichment
shifts.note: Annual Instinct cadence (MI350 → MI355X → MI450) is a structural moat vs NVDA's Blackwell

AMD.verdict: Story improving on every dimension — re-acceleration, margin expansion, China partial reopening. The OpenAI MI450 commitment is the thesis-strengthening event that confirms AMD as a credible #2 GPU platform.

AMKR.headline: Revenue re-accelerated sharply to +27.5% in Q1 FY26; capex quadrupling in 2026 for Arizona advanced-packaging fab.

shifts.trend: FY25 revenue +6% YoY → Q1 FY26 +27.5% YoY across all four end markets
shifts.note: Broadest acceleration since AI-packaging demand emerged

shifts.trend: Actual capex $904.6M (2025) → guided $2.5–3.0B (2026); Q1 capex $225M vs $80M prior Q1
shifts.note: Arizona Facility construction is the primary driver; US advanced-packaging exposure from scratch

shifts.trend: Apple ~30% and Qualcomm ~11% of revenue — unchanged across both filings
shifts.note: Customer concentration stable but watch Apple for any advanced-packaging share decisions

AMKR.verdict: Thesis intact and executing: Amkor is funding the capex wave to capture US advanced-packaging demand. The key risk is whether $2.5–3.0B capex translates to revenue before debt leverage rises.

AMZN.headline: AWS growth re-accelerated to +28% in Q1; capex annualizing at $170B+ pace; AWS RPO surged $120B in one quarter to $364B.

shifts.note: Re-acceleration validates AI workload conversion after FY25 optimization headwind

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $128.3B → Q1 FY26 alone $43.2B (annualizing >$170B); mgmt guided further increases in FY26
shifts.note: Single quarter capex now 34% of full-year 2025

shifts.trend: AWS RPO: $244B (FY25) → $364B (Q1 FY26), +$120B in one quarter on $100B OpenAI expansion deal
shifts.note: RPO doubling implies multi-year committed revenue not yet in income statement

shifts.trend: AWS operating margin 37.7% in Q1 FY26 vs prior year comparable ~30%
shifts.note: Margin expansion despite accelerating capex — highly favorable unit economics

AMZN.verdict: AWS growth re-acceleration plus RPO surge are the strongest dual signal in the hyperscaler cohort; Amazon is the clearest demand signal for the inference stack with $364B of contracted cloud revenue.

ANET.headline: Growth re-accelerated to +35% in Q1 FY26; product deferred revenue swelling to $6.2B as AI Ethernet trials convert to long-term contracts.

shifts.trend: FY25 +28.6% → Q1 FY26 +35.1% YoY as Cloud Titan AI Ethernet deployments expand
shifts.note: Contracts still in acceptance period inflate deferred — revenue recognition lag vs underlying demand

shifts.trend: Product deferred revenue: $5.37B (FY25 10-K) → $6.20B (Q1 FY26); binding backlog $968M
shifts.note: Deferred build is the leading indicator for revenue recognition in coming quarters

shifts.trend: Cloud and AI Titans 48% of FY25 revenue; customer mix stable QoQ
shifts.note: Two customers >10% — similar to FY25 pattern

ANET.verdict: Deferred-revenue build is the key forward indicator — $6.2B in deferred provides earnings visibility that the reported revenue number understates. Growth thesis intact and accelerating.

AVGO.headline: Revenue growth re-accelerating to +29% with semis +52%; top-5 customer concentration tightened from 40% to ~50% of revenue.

shifts.trend: FY25 +24% → Q1 FY26 +29%; Semiconductor solutions Q1 +52% YoY on custom AI XPUs and AI networking
shifts.note: XPU custom ASICs (Google TPU, Meta MTIA, Apple) driving the most explosive growth

shifts.trend: Top-5 end customers: ~40% of FY25 revenue → ~50% of Q1 FY26 revenue; one distributor 32% → 42%
shifts.note: Hyperscaler concentration rising — makes revenue more binary by customer

shifts.trend: AI networking (Tomahawk, Jericho) bundled with XPU wins is raising per-customer wallet share
shifts.note: Infrastructure software segment ($21B ARR) provides stability vs semi cyclicality

AVGO.verdict: Re-acceleration is real but the customer-concentration disclosure is the new risk factor — at 50% top-5, a single hyperscaler capex cycle shift would significantly impact revenue.

CAT.headline: Backlog doubled to $51.2B on data-center power demand; revenue growing low-double-digit with Power & Energy +22% guidance confirmed.

shifts.trend: Order backlog: $30.0B (end-FY24) → $51.2B (end-FY25) → still elevated in Q1 FY26; largest component is Power & Energy data center gensets
shifts.note: 6–8 quarter delivery lead time provides multi-year revenue visibility

shifts.trend: Power & Energy sales YoY: +32% (FY25) → +22% (Q1 FY26, on a higher base)
shifts.note: Slightly decelerating growth rate but on a base that grew 32% — absolute growth absolute growth still expanding

shifts.trend: FY26 guidance low-double-digit revenue growth confirmed in both 10-K and Q1 10-Q; tariff costs guided $2.2–2.4B and reaffirmed
shifts.note: Consistency of guidance in volatile macro is a positive signal

CAT.verdict: Thesis intact — data-center genset demand is producing a structural backlog re-rate. The main watchpoint is tariff cost absorption ($2.2–2.4B FY26) and whether large-turbine lead times can extend the backlog rather than normalize.

CDNS.headline: Revenue growth inflected from +14% to +19%; China unexpectedly accelerating +36% despite BIS restrictions, driving backlog to $8.0B.

shifts.trend: FY25 +14% → Q1 FY26 +19% YoY; backlog $7.8B → $8.0B
shifts.note: Backlog growth implies forward revenue visibility extending

shifts.trend: China revenue: +19% (FY25) → +36% (Q1 FY26, $189M); counter to BIS export-control narrative
shifts.note: Chinese fabs may be front-loading EDA licenses ahead of potential restrictions — watch sustainability

shifts.trend: Both Digital IC and Custom IC/PCB growing; no segment concentration shift disclosed
shifts.note: Product backlog composition not broken out — hard to attribute by segment

CDNS.verdict: China strength is the counterintuitive standout and the thesis-testing variable — if sustainable, the consensus China-risk discount is too punitive; if front-loading, it will reverse. Watch Q2 for sustainability.

CEG.headline: Calpine $22B acquisition closed Jan 7, 2026 — Constellation doubles to 55 GW becoming world's largest private power producer; Crane (TMI) restart secured DOE $1B loan.

shifts.trend: Portfolio: ~25 GW nuclear (pre-acquisition) → 55 GW (post-Calpine, adds gas, geothermal, solar)
shifts.note: From pure-nuclear play to diversified clean/dispatchable power — structural risk/return profile change

shifts.trend: $22B acquisition financed partly with equity/debt; leverage rising vs pre-deal balance sheet
shifts.note: FCF yield compressed near-term; returns improve as Calpine synergies materialize

shifts.trend: Revenue base doubles post-Calpine; Meta 20-year PPA (Q3) + Crane restart (10-K) add contracted long-duration demand
shifts.note: Data-center PPA pipeline underpins multi-decade baseload demand

CEG.verdict: Transformational deal closed — CEG is no longer just a nuclear pure-play; the Calpine integration and leverage profile are now the primary investment risk, but the contracted PPA pipeline (data centers) is the thesis.

CLS.headline: Revenue +53% YoY in Q1 FY26, continuing FY25's +42% pace; HPS (hyperscaler networking systems) growing fastest at +76%.

shifts.trend: FY25 total revenue +42% → Q1 FY26 +53% YoY; CCS +76%, HPS +63%
shifts.note: Q1 run-rate implies 2026 revenue well ahead of FY25 pace

shifts.trend: Historical capex ~1.5–2% of revenue → FY26 guided ~$1B (~6% of revenue) to support hyperscaler demand
shifts.note: Capex step-up is the key leverage point — watch free-cash-flow generation

shifts.trend: Adjusted operating margin 8.0% (Q1 actual vs 7.8% prior guide mid-point); HPS mix benefit expected to improve long-term margin
shifts.note: Still thin margins typical of EMS, but richer HPS mix is the margin expansion path

CLS.verdict: Celestica is executing well on hyperscaler demand; the capex step-up (1.5% → 6%) is the key watchpoint — strong execution justifies it, but it compresses near-term FCF and is a binary bet on sustained demand.

CMI.headline: Power Systems EBITDA margin expanded 680bps to 29.5% in Q1; data-center demand sustaining with backlog stretching 6–8 quarters.

shifts.trend: Power Systems EBITDA margin: 22.7% (FY25) → 29.5% (Q1 FY26), +680bps in one quarter
shifts.note: Operating leverage from data-center power generation mix — historically a low-margin segment

shifts.trend: Power Systems sales: +19% YoY (Q1 FY26) on data-center-driven North America and China demand
shifts.note: FY25 Power Systems EBITDA +44% at 22.7% margin — re-acceleration continuing into Q1

shifts.trend: Both 10-K and Q1 10-Q maintain 'strong Power Systems demand' language; no backlog easing language added
shifts.note: 6–8 quarter backlog visibility maintained — no softening signal

CMI.verdict: Operational leverage is the standout — 680bps margin expansion in one quarter is exceptional for a historically cyclical industrial segment. Data-center backlog duration (6–8 quarters) is the durability signal.

COHR.headline: Two consecutive quarters of re-acceleration; Datacom & Comms segment +41% in FQ3 with margin +49%; 'scale across' hyperscalers adds new growth vector.

shifts.trend: Total revenue YoY: +17% (FQ2) → +21% (FQ3); D&C segment: +34% → +41%
shifts.note: Re-acceleration in both headline and the core AI datacenter segment

shifts.trend: D&C segment profit: +44% (FQ2) → +49% (FQ3); overall gross margin +140bps (FQ2) with continued expansion in FQ3
shifts.note: 1.6T cycle is structurally re-rating Coherent's margin profile beyond cyclical normalization

shifts.trend: 'Scale across' a new phrase in FQ3 suggesting additional hyperscaler customers or deployments beyond initial 800G→1.6T wins
shifts.note: Incremental demand signal — implies supply allocation across more hyperscaler deployments

COHR.verdict: Both the growth rate and the margin profile are accelerating simultaneously — the 1.6T transition is delivering structural uplift, not just cyclical recovery. Two clean quarters of re-acceleration make this a high-conviction signal.

CRM.headline: Revenue growth stable at 9–10%; RPO recovered from Q3 dip to $72.4B +14% at fiscal year-end; Informatica $9.6B closes the Agentforce data gap.

shifts.trend: Revenue YoY: +9% (Q3 FY26) → +10% (FY26 full year); not accelerating despite Agentforce launch
shifts.note: AI monetization still nascent — revenue impact not yet visible at the top line

shifts.trend: cRPO: declined in Q3 → recovered to $35.1B +16% at FY26 year-end; total RPO $59.5B (Q3) → $72.4B (FY26)
shifts.note: RPO recovery from Q3 dip is a healthy sign — not sustained contraction

shifts.trend: AI/Agents/Slack platform grew +19% YoY (the fastest-growing bucket); Agentforce 360 + Informatica closing data-cloud gap
shifts.note: $9.6B Informatica deal completes the agentic-AI data-layer that was the gap vs ServiceNow

CRM.verdict: Story is transitional — not broken but not yet inflecting; the Informatica deal is strategically right, and RPO recovery suggests no demand signal breakage. Watch FY27 for AI monetization to show up in revenue acceleration.

CRWV.headline: Revenue +112% YoY in Q1; Microsoft concentration dropped from 67% to 45% as Meta (20%) and others scale; RPO surged $38B to $98.8B in one quarter.

shifts.trend: Revenue YoY: +168% (FY25) → +112% (Q1 FY26); absolute revenue $5.1B (FY25) → $2.08B (Q1 alone)
shifts.note: Deceleration is base-effect — absolute quarter revenue implies $8B+ annualized run-rate, well ahead of FY25

shifts.trend: Microsoft share: 67% (FY25) → 45% (Q1 FY26); Meta at 20% is new disclosed customer
shifts.note: Diversification reduces binary Microsoft dependency — a material derisking event

shifts.trend: RPO: $60.7B (FY25 10-K) → $98.8B (Q1 FY26), +$38.1B in one quarter; 850 MW active power growing
shifts.note: $98.8B RPO vs $8B annualized run-rate implies 12+ year backlog — monetization pace is the primary risk

shifts.trend: Total debt $21.6B at ~10% avg rate; Q1 added $7.8B debt+equity — leverage metrics tightening
shifts.note: Interest burden vs revenue ratio bears watching as new debt comes online

CRWV.verdict: RPO surge and concentration easing are thesis-positive; the $21.6B debt load at 10% is the structural risk — CRWV needs sustained high revenue growth to service it. Microsoft dependency falling makes the credit story more defensible.

CSCO.headline: Revenue growth re-accelerating from +8% to +10% on consecutive quarterly uplifts; Networking led the acceleration with +21% in Q2.

shifts.trend: Total revenue YoY: +8% (Q1 FY26) → +10% (Q2 FY26); product revenue H1 FY26 +12%
shifts.note: Consistent with cloud titan AI infrastructure order conversion

shifts.trend: Networking YoY: +15% (Q1) → +21% (Q2) on AI Infrastructure and Campus demand
shifts.note: Silicon One capacity secured via purchase commitments in Q1 — supply locked for H2 ramp

shifts.trend: Inventory and purchase commitments +9% in Q1 as CSCO secured Silicon One capacity; backlog not disclosed but demand language consistent
shifts.note: Proactive inventory build suggests confidence in sustained H2 demand

CSCO.verdict: Two consecutive quarters of acceleration in both total revenue and Networking validates the AI infrastructure story; the Silicon One capacity lock is a supply-chain confidence signal for H2 delivery.

ETN.headline: EA Q1 orders +42% organic with book-to-bill 1.2x; Americas backlog +44% YoY; $9.5B Boyd Thermal acquisition adds liquid cooling exposure.

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas backlog: $19.8B total company (FY25) → $14.5B Americas alone +44% YoY in Q1; book-to-bill 1.2x
shifts.note: Backlog growing faster than revenue — capacity expansion is now the binding constraint

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas organic orders +42% (Q1 FY26) vs +16% segment growth (FY25)
shifts.note: Order re-acceleration in Q1 exceeds prior-year full-year pace

shifts.trend: $9.5B Boyd Thermal acquisition announced (FY25 10-K) to add liquid cooling exposure; close expected Q2 2026
shifts.note: Strategic pivot toward liquid cooling — EATON positioned across power (Eaton) + thermal (Boyd) for data center

ETN.verdict: Eaton's data-center order momentum is compounding — orders growing faster than revenue means future quarters have high visibility. Boyd Thermal adds liquid cooling at exactly the right inflection point.

FN.headline: Revenue growth accelerating from +35.9% to +39.3%; DCI hyperscaler interconnect +90.4% in FQ3 as 1.6T-cycle pull-through intensifies.

shifts.trend: Total revenue YoY: +35.9% (FQ2) → +39.3% (FQ3); DCI: +90.4% in FQ3 vs implied ~65% in FQ2
shifts.note: DCI is now the fastest-growing segment — hyperscaler 1.6T cycle scaling

shifts.trend: Telecom: +28.7% (FQ2) → +42.5% (FQ3); non-optical HPC growing +61.1% (FQ2)
shifts.note: Multiple growth vectors accelerating simultaneously — not a single-customer pull-forward

shifts.trend: DCI backlog/demand commentary: 1.6T-cycle described as 'accelerating' across both quarters; no easing language added
shifts.note: Fabrinet's contract-manufacturing model means visibility is tied to customer purchase orders

FN.verdict: Fabrinet is a high-fidelity read-through on 1.6T demand — two consecutive quarters of acceleration with DCI +90% in the most recent print. Clean inference-stack exposure with minimal China risk.

GEV.headline: RPO accelerating from +26% to +32% YoY; Gas Power EBITDA +57% in Q1; heavy-duty turbine bookings tripled since 2023.

shifts.trend: Total RPO: $150B +26% YoY (FY25) → $163.3B +32% YoY (Q1 FY26), +$13.3B in one quarter
shifts.note: RPO growing faster than revenue — demand piling up faster than GEV can deliver

shifts.trend: RPO YoY growth: +26% (FY25) → +32% (Q1 FY26); Gas Power EBITDA +57% in Q1
shifts.note: Heavy-duty turbine bookings tripled 2023→2025 (41 → 110) — the slope is still rising

shifts.trend: HA-turbines 43 deliveries in FY25; Q1 FY26 Gas Power EBITDA +57% on HD turbine deliveries and favorable pricing
shifts.note: Pricing power improving as order book grows — pricing leverage from scarcity

GEV.verdict: RPO acceleration into Q1 confirms that data-center power demand is not plateauing — it's compounding. GEV has multi-year visibility and pricing leverage; the key risk is manufacturing capacity to convert the $163B RPO.

GNRC.headline: Revenue inflected from -2% to +12%; C&I segment +28% driven by data centers; acquired Enercon for hyperscale custom power solutions.

shifts.trend: FY25 revenue -2% YoY → Q1 FY26 +12% YoY as C&I segment offsets residential softness
shifts.note: C&I segment crossing 28% growth while residential remains subdued — mix shift accelerating

shifts.trend: C&I segment: +28% (Q1 FY26) on data center generator demand; Enercon acquisition adds hyperscale-specific large-megawatt power
shifts.note: GNRC launching large-megawatt diesel gensets specifically for data centers — new product line

shifts.trend: Enercon acquisition expands hyperscale custom-power addressable market vs prior product limitations
shifts.note: Inorganic M&A to extend into datacenter-specific large-power applications

GNRC.verdict: Inflection confirmed — GNRC is successfully pivoting from a residential-utility company toward a C&I/data-center power play. The question is whether the C&I mix can exceed 50%+ to sustainably re-rate the multiple.

GOOGL.headline: Google Cloud re-accelerated to +63% YoY in Q1 with operating income growing 3x; capex annualizing >$140B; revenue backlog nearly doubled to $467.6B.

shifts.trend: Google Cloud YoY: +36% (FY25) → +63% (Q1 FY26); Cloud operating income: $13.9B (FY25) → $6.6B in Q1 alone
shifts.note: $6.6B Q1 op income annualizes to $26.4B vs $13.9B FY25 — profit-growth accelerating faster than revenue

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $91.4B → Q1 FY26 $35.7B (annualizing $143B+); mgmt: 'significantly increase' from $91.4B confirmed
shifts.note: TPU supply-deal capex driving a structural step-up

shifts.trend: Revenue backlog: $242.8B (FY25) → $467.6B (Q1 FY26), +$224.8B in one quarter — 93% surge
shifts.note: Multi-GW TPU supply deals signed in Q1 — largest contracted revenue expansion in Google Cloud history

shifts.trend: Cloud op margin: ~24% (FY25) → ~33% (Q1 FY26) — structural expansion on scale
shifts.note: Cloud profitability scaling at hyperscale as fixed costs are absorbed

GOOGL.verdict: Google Cloud's Q1 re-acceleration to +63% is the strongest growth print since 2022; the backlog near-doubling in one quarter is extraordinary and implies secured multi-year demand. This is thesis-strengthening for every layer of the inference stack.

HPE.headline: Revenue growth +18.4% in Q1 on Juniper full-quarter; Networking +151.5% (Juniper inflated); Cloud & AI segment -2.7% YoY on margin-dilutive AI servers.

shifts.trend: Total revenue YoY: +13.8% (FY25) → +18.4% (Q1 FY26); Networking YoY: +51.1% (FY25 partial Juniper) → +151.5% (Q1 full Juniper)
shifts.note: Networking headline optically elevated by full-quarter Juniper vs partial-quarter prior year — organic trajectory cleaner at FY25 +51% pace

shifts.trend: Cloud & AI -2.7% YoY; GAAP operating loss $437M in FY25 on $1.6B goodwill impairment; AI server margins dilutive on GPU pass-through pricing
shifts.note: AI server growth is a revenue headwind to margin — volume-at-cost model

shifts.trend: Juniper synergies $600M by FY28 (reaffirmed); Networking-as-a-Service ARR growing
shifts.note: Long synergy timeline means Juniper margin benefit is multi-year, not near-term

HPE.verdict: Mixed picture: Networking growth is real but partially inflated by Juniper comparisons; the Cloud & AI margin headwind from AI servers is structural until GPU economics normalize. $600M Juniper synergies are multi-year — patience required.

HUBB.headline: Revenue growth accelerating from +7% to +11% on data-center and T&D strength; backlog $2.16B +14% supports multi-quarter visibility.

shifts.trend: Electrical Solutions organic: +7.1% (FY25) → +10.6% (Q1 FY26); total company: low single-digit FY25 → +11% Q1
shifts.note: Consistent acceleration across both electrical and utility segments

shifts.trend: Total backlog: $2.16B +14% (FY25); sustained through Q1 FY26 with Electrical and Utility both growing
shifts.note: Dual-tailwind from data-center (electrical) and grid upgrade (utility) sustaining backlog

shifts.trend: Utility Solutions organic +6.8% (Q1 FY26) alongside Electrical organic +10.6% — both segments accelerating
shifts.note: $958M acquisitions in FY25 expanding product reach into both end-markets

HUBB.verdict: Steady compounder accelerating on two parallel demand drivers (data center + grid); backlog provides near-term visibility. Not a high-beta play but a reliable infrastructure spend beneficiary.

INTC.headline: Revenue inflected from -0.5% to +7% on DCAI +22%; Foundry operating loss $2.4B in Q1; $4.1B restructuring charge signals ongoing transformation pain.

shifts.trend: FY25 revenue -0.5% → Q1 FY26 +7% YoY; DCAI (Data Center and AI) segment +22% in Q1
shifts.note: DCAI inflection on Gaudi AI accelerators and Xeon SP share recovery

shifts.trend: Intel Foundry operating loss: $10.3B (FY25) → $2.4B (Q1 FY26 — one quarter); external foundry revenue still only $307M
shifts.note: Sequential loss improvement is real but Foundry is still deeply loss-making; 18A ramp critical in 2026

shifts.trend: $4.1B Q1 restructuring + net loss $3.7B in Q1; SoftBank ($5B) and NVIDIA ($2.0B) investments improve liquidity but dilute equity
shifts.note: Restructuring magnitude in a single quarter signals structural cost issues not yet resolved

INTC.verdict: Revenue inflection is real and DCAI recovery is the bright spot; however, the Foundry transformation is multi-year and costly — Intel remains a high-variance turnaround story with execution risk concentrated in 18A yields and customer wins.

KLAC.headline: Record quarter at $3.42B (+11%); Korea +80% on HBM/DRAM investment; China declining but HBM memory investment more than offsets.

shifts.trend: Revenue YoY: +7% (Q2 FY26) → +11% (Q3 FY26, record); QoQ also growing
shifts.note: Two consecutive quarter accelerations — HBM investment cycle is the driver

shifts.trend: China as % of revenue: ~36% (pre-restriction) → 30% (Q2 FY26) → ~28–30% (Q3 FY26)
shifts.note: China share declining but not collapsing; memory restrictions specific, not total

shifts.trend: Korea (SK Hynix/Samsung HBM) +80% YoY in Q3; Taiwan -12% on shipment timing (not demand loss)
shifts.note: Korea strength validates HBM-driven capex is real and sustained; Taiwan timing delta is not a demand signal

KLAC.verdict: Record quarter driven by HBM investment is the cleanest thesis validator in the WFE space; Korea +80% is extraordinary. China declining is managed/expected; gross-margin compression from rising DRAM chip costs in imaging computers is the only near-term headwind.

LEU.headline: Backlog grew incrementally to $3.9B; Fluor EPC contract signed for Piketon HALEU expansion; DOE FY27 budget gap is a key execution risk.

shifts.trend: Backlog: $3.8B (FY25) → $3.9B (Q1 FY26, incl. $2.4B contingent); $900M DOE HALEU task order anchors the near-term build
shifts.note: HALEU backlog contingent on cascade funding not yet appropriated for FY27

shifts.trend: Backlog growing modestly (+$100M); first revenues from HALEU not until cascade completion, likely CY2027–2028
shifts.note: Pre-revenue stage for HALEU — current revenues still from legacy SWU contracts

shifts.trend: Fluor EPC contract signed for Piketon expansion (Feb 2026); Oklo JV exploring HALEU deconversion co-development
shifts.note: Two execution milestones in the window: EPC signed + JV letter of intent with Oklo

LEU.verdict: Steady execution but pre-revenue on HALEU — the DOE FY27 budget gap (cascade funding not included) is the thesis risk that could delay the monetization timeline by 1–2 years.

LITE.headline: Revenue accelerated to +90.1% in FQ3; gross margin re-rated structurally from 36.1% to 44.2% across two quarters as the 1.6T cycle drives pricing power.

shifts.trend: Revenue YoY: +65.5% (FQ2) → +90.1% (FQ3); cloud transceiver revenue up $137M QoQ
shifts.note: Acceleration in a quarter where year-ago comps were already elevated — real demand pull

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP gross margin: 36.1% (FQ2, +1,130bps YoY) → 44.2% (FQ3, +1,540bps YoY); structurally re-rating
shifts.note: 800bps of gross margin expansion in a single quarter is rare — 200G-lane mix at scale is transformational

shifts.trend: Laser chip/InP components: +68.3% (FQ2) → ~$137M cloud transceiver uplift (FQ3); 200G-lane (1.6T building block) ramp continues
shifts.note: Supply is gating — laser chip availability is the constraint cited in both quarters

LITE.verdict: Lumentum is executing the highest-quality print in the optics cohort — +90% growth and +1,540bps margin expansion simultaneously. The 1.6T structural re-rate is the thesis-defining event; the risk is supply-side (laser chip) constraints limiting how fast the margin expansion can compound.

LRCX.headline: Revenue accelerating QoQ (+9%); DRAM/HBM memory mix expanding from 34% to 39%; China stable at 35%; WFE growth expected to continue in CY2026.

shifts.trend: Revenue: $5.34B (Q2 FY26) → $5.84B (Q3 FY26), +9% QoQ; H1 YoY was +25%
shifts.note: Sequential re-acceleration implies HBM/DRAM capex pulling forward into H1 CY2026

shifts.trend: Memory (primarily DRAM/HBM) mix: ~34% (Q2) → 39% (Q3); Foundry: 59% → 54%
shifts.note: Memory mix gaining at foundry's expense as HBM investment from MU/SK/Samsung accelerates

shifts.trend: China revenue: 35% of revenue (both Q2 and Q3 FY26); no significant change
shifts.note: China share stable post-October 2023 restriction — remaining at 35% despite restrictions implies non-restricted tool categories

shifts.trend: Guidance language upgraded from 'continued growth' to 'continued WFE growth in CY2026 with AI driving higher semi spending'
shifts.note: More explicit AI attribution added to guidance language across the two quarters

LRCX.verdict: HBM-driven mix shift is the thesis driver — memory growing as a % of WFE is bullish for Lam's etch/deposition intensity advantage in DRAM stacks. China at 35% remains a material binary risk if further restrictions are imposed.

META.headline: Revenue re-accelerated to +33% YoY in Q1; FY26 capex raised to $125–145B; non-cancelable commitments $237.7B signal sustained AI infrastructure demand.

shifts.trend: Revenue YoY: +22% (FY25) → +33% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: AI-driven ad personalization re-acceleration validating the AI monetization thesis

shifts.trend: FY26 capex guidance: $115–135B (10-K) → $125–145B (Q1 10-Q); FY25 actual $72.2B
shifts.note: Two consecutive upward revisions in one year — demand conviction increasing

shifts.trend: Non-cancelable commitments $237.7B including cloud capacity; AI infrastructure commitments growing faster than revenues
shifts.note: Committed spend growing faster than revenue = bullish for hyperscaler supplier base

shifts.trend: Reality Labs op loss $19.2B (FY25) — expected similar in FY26; AI/ad revenue offsetting RL investment
shifts.note: RL is a drag but not worsening; AI efficiency gains in core ad business are funding RL

META.verdict: Meta is executing the most convincing AI-to-revenue conversion in the hyperscaler cohort — +33% growth + capex raise is the optimal combination for inference-stack demand. Two capex raises in two filings is a strong signal.

MOD.headline: Climate Solutions data-center segment grew $130M YoY in Q3; Performance Technology spin-off/merger with Gentherm announced — Modine becomes pure-play DC cooling.

shifts.trend: Climate Solutions data-center revenue: +$67M YoY (Q2 FY26) → +$130M YoY (Q3 FY26); segment total +51% in Q3
shifts.note: Data center contribution nearly doubling in one quarter — accelerating not slowing

shifts.trend: Performance Technology spin-off/merger with Gentherm (Reverse Morris Trust) announced Q3 — Modine to become pure-play data center thermal company
shifts.note: Strategic clarity is a re-rating catalyst — pure-play DC cooling multiple higher than diversified industrial

shifts.trend: Q2 margins -440bps from US capacity ramp costs; Q3 commentary suggests ramp headwinds beginning to moderate as US production stabilizes
shifts.note: Initial ramp margin compression is expected to normalize as utilization rises

MOD.verdict: The Gentherm spin is the thesis-defining catalyst — Modine is becoming a pure-play data center thermal company, which justifies a structural multiple re-rate. Execution risk is the RMT timeline (expected end of CY2026) and US ramp cost normalization.

MRVL.headline: Data center revenue grew from +38% to +46% YoY reaching $6.1B (74% of total); two strategic acquisitions (Celestial AI, XConn) closed post year-end to extend photonic and CXL/PCIe.

shifts.trend: Total revenue YoY: +37% (Q3 FY26) → +42% (FY26 full year); DC segment: +38% (Q3) → +46% (FY26)
shifts.note: Re-acceleration from Q3 to full-year pace driven by custom ASIC ramp completion

shifts.trend: DC share of revenue: 73% (Q3 FY26) → 74% (FY26); electro-optics within DC growing as % of DC
shifts.note: Marvell is becoming a DC-only business — 74% of revenue from one end-market

shifts.trend: Post year-end: Celestial AI (up to $5.5B, photonic fabric) + XConn (CXL/PCIe switching) closed; no revenue contribution in FY26
shifts.note: Two acquisitions adding photonic interconnect and CXL memory fabric — positioning for next-generation DC architecture

MRVL.verdict: Growth accelerating into FY26 + transformative acquisitions both support the thesis; at 74% DC concentration, any hyperscaler capex slowdown is highly impactful. China export-restriction risk on existing customers is the key near-term watchpoint.

MSFT.headline: Microsoft Cloud growth re-accelerated from +26% to +29%; Azure +39% to +40%; commercial RPO held at $625–627B; Q3 capex $30.9B.

shifts.trend: Microsoft Cloud YoY: +26% (Q2) → +29% (Q3); Azure: +39% (Q2) → +40% (Q3)
shifts.note: Azure re-acceleration validates AI workload conversion after optimization cycle — the key thesis test

shifts.trend: Q2 capex $29.9B → Q3 capex $30.9B; H1 total $49.3B; run-rate implies $120B+ annualized
shifts.note: Sequential increase in per-quarter capex — investment pace not plateauing

shifts.trend: Commercial RPO: $625B (Q2) → $627B (Q3) — stable at historic high; 99%+ growth YoY in Q2 comparison
shifts.note: RPO plateau at $627B after massive expansion — monetization of prior signings now the focus

shifts.trend: Gross margins remain compressed by AI infrastructure investments; cloud margins holding as AI monetization begins
shifts.note: AI revenue ramping but gross margin impact from GPU-intensive workloads being absorbed

MSFT.verdict: Azure re-acceleration to +40% is the thesis-confirming event — concerns about AI workload translation into durable Azure growth are now resolved across two consecutive prints. RPO plateau is not a decline signal.

MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

MXL.headline: Revenue growth accelerating from +30% to +43% YoY; infrastructure (optical DSPs) leading recovery; 800G→1.6T transition explicitly cited as next growth vector.

shifts.trend: FY25 +30% YoY → Q1 FY26 +43% YoY; infrastructure segment +$36.3M QoQ
shifts.note: Recovery from CY2024 inventory correction fully behind; 800G→1.6T transition pulling demand forward

shifts.trend: Infrastructure (optical DSPs for 800G→1.6T) is the highest-growth segment; broadband +$87.6M (FY25)
shifts.note: Two structural growth drivers: 800G datacenter transceivers + DSL/DOCSIS broadband refresh

shifts.trend: Top customer: 13% (Q1 FY26) vs higher in prior years; top-10 at 56% — diversifying
shifts.note: Broader customer base forming around optical DSP designs at multiple transceiver ODMs

MXL.verdict: MXL is riding two secular tailwinds (1.6T and broadband) with a diversifying customer base; at $137M quarterly revenue it's a high-beta/smaller-cap play — re-acceleration is real but execution risk (inventory cycles) remains elevated.

NOW.headline: Consistent 20–22% revenue growth maintained across both periods; RPO $27.7–28.2B range; AI monetization via consumption-priced Now Assist embedded across platform.

shifts.trend: Revenue YoY: +21% (FY25) → +22% (Q1 FY26); subscription +22% (Q1) consistent with FY25 pace
shifts.note: Steady-state 20%+ growth not accelerating but not decelerating either — quality compounder
RPO slight dip is normal seasonality; year-over-year growth rate stable at 25–27%

shifts.trend: Now Assist AI agents embedded in ITSM/CSM/HRSD; consumption pricing creates an upsell layer on top of base subscriptions
shifts.note: Consumption pricing is the AI monetization lever — early-stage revenue contribution but growing

NOW.verdict: ServiceNow is a high-quality compounder executing consistently; AI monetization via Now Assist is the incremental upside catalyst not yet fully in estimates. No red flags — watch for the consumption pricing to show up in NRR acceleration.

NVDA.headline: DC growth holding ~+66–68% YoY; customer concentration formally disclosed at 4-way >10%; China is now effectively zero, but supply/energy commentary unchanged.

shifts.note: Slight re-acceleration on a full-year basis after Blackwell ramp
shifts.trend: DC YoY +66% (Q3 FY26) → +68% (FY26 full year); Networking +162% on GB200/GB300 NVLink ramp

shifts.note: Hyperscaler dependence newly visible — diligence risk-additive
shifts.trend: Q3 disclosed 4 direct customers each >10% of revenue (22/15/13/11%); 10-K did not previously break this out

shifts.note: China optionality fully impaired across both filings
shifts.trend: Q3 China $3.0B vs $8.1B prior-year Q3; H20 effectively zero by FY26 after $4.5B Q1 inventory charge; Feb 2026 H200 license carries 25% US import tariff
Non-GAAP GM 73.4% (Q3) → 73.5% guide (Q1 FY27); compressed YoY on Blackwell full-system mix but holding within target

shifts.note: Quote nearly identical across the two filings
shifts.trend: Both filings cite data-center / energy / capital availability as the gating constraint on customer deployments — no easing language added

shifts.note: Vendor-financing footprint growing — watch FCF translation
shifts.trend: FY26 10-K disclosed $17.5B in private/infra investments plus $3.5B land/power/shell guarantees to early-stage AI customers; Q3 added $7.5B new DC lease obligations FY26 Q4–FY30

NVDA.verdict: Story still about supply-constrained AI capex. The new disclosures (4-way customer concentration, vendor-financing footprint) are risk-additive but don't break the thesis; China optionality is now fully impaired and should be modeled at zero.

NVT.headline: Revenue growth accelerated from +30% to +53.5%; organic growth from +13% to +34.4% — the largest organic re-acceleration in the cohort; Systems Protection +76%.

shifts.trend: Total revenue YoY: +30% (FY25) → +53.5% (Q1 FY26); organic: +13% (FY25) → +34.4% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Organic re-acceleration of +21pts is exceptional — Avail EPG acquisition not the sole driver

shifts.trend: Systems Protection (enclosures + liquid cooling): +42% (FY25) → +76% (Q1 FY26); segment margin 22.7%
shifts.note: Liquid cooling specifically called out as growing fastest within Systems Protection

shifts.trend: Backlog $2.3B (FY25) growing; data center cited as primary driver with multi-quarter forward visibility
shifts.note: Organic acceleration implies demand pulling ahead of prior backlog conversion expectations

NVT.verdict: The organic re-acceleration from +13% to +34% is the key signal — this isn't just acquisition math; nVent is winning data-center enclosure and liquid-cooling contracts at an accelerating pace.

OKLO.headline: Meta 1.2 GW hyperscaler anchor deal signed Jan 2026; order book ~18,100 MWe; first powerhouse deployment targeted 2028 with $350–450M 2026 capex.

shifts.trend: Order book: ~15,600 MWe (Q3 FY25) → ~18,100 MWe (10-K period); Meta 1.2 GW is the quality anchor
shifts.note: Meta deal transforms order book from speculative to hyperscaler-validated

shifts.trend: FY25 operating cash burn ~$65M → FY26 guided operating $80–100M + investing $350–450M; step-up into deployment
shifts.note: Capex step-up signals transition from R&D to commercialization phase

shifts.trend: Meta 1.2 GW is now the dominant single order; no other hyperscaler contracts disclosed as firm
shifts.note: Single large customer as anchor creates concentration risk but also valuation credibility

OKLO.verdict: Meta deal is thesis-validating — a quality hyperscaler putting firm capital toward Aurora deployment legitimizes the order book. Execution risk is fully concentrated in the 2028 deployment timeline; any NRC licensing delays are the primary downside.

ORCL.headline: OCI growth re-accelerated from +68% to +84%; RPO grew from $523B to $552B; Q3 capex surged to $18.7B — largest single-quarter capex in Oracle's history.

shifts.trend: OCI YoY: +68% (Q2 FY26) → +84% (Q3 FY26); total cloud +45% in Q3
shifts.note: Re-acceleration in both directions — the fastest OCI growth print to date

shifts.trend: H1 capex $20.5B → 9-month capex $39.2B → Q3 alone ~$18.7B; $42.7B debt + $5B mandatory convert issued for funding
shifts.note: Q3 capex is >90% of full-year H1 pace — a structural step-up, not a one-time event

shifts.trend: RPO: $523.3B (Q2) → $552.6B (Q3), +$29.3B sequentially; still growing despite massive delivery obligations
shifts.note: RPO growth exceeding capex spend implies new contracts arriving faster than old ones are being monetized

shifts.trend: $42.7B senior notes + $5B mandatory convertible issued in Q3; total debt reaching historic levels
shifts.note: Leverage rising rapidly but management indicates RPO is contracted — debt is funded by committed future revenue

ORCL.verdict: OCI re-acceleration to +84% is exceptional; the risk is whether Oracle can convert $552B RPO on schedule given the Q3 capex surge required to scale. Leverage is high but partially backed by contractual revenue — execution is everything.

PLTR.headline: Revenue re-accelerated to +85% YoY in Q1; commercial +95% — now growing faster than government; operating income $754M in one quarter vs $1.41B full-year FY25.

shifts.trend: Total revenue YoY: +53% (FY25) → +85% (Q1 FY26); commercial: +53% → +95%; government: faster but commercial now leading
shifts.note: Commercial crossing government in growth rate is a structural shift — enterprise AI adoption scaling

shifts.trend: Operating income: $1.41B full-year FY25 → $754M in Q1 FY26 alone (annualizing $3.0B+); operating margin ~46%
shifts.note: Operating leverage is extraordinary — revenue growing faster than costs

shifts.trend: AIP (Artificial Intelligence Platform) driving commercial wins at pace; Foundry government platform still growing at 76%
shifts.note: Government platform sustaining while commercial surges — no trade-off between the two segments

PLTR.verdict: The commercial acceleration to +95% is the thesis-confirming event — Palantir's AIP is achieving the enterprise AI adoption inflection point. Operating leverage at 46% margin makes this one of the most profitable high-growth software businesses at scale.

PWR.headline: Revenue stable at +26% YoY; backlog compounding from $44B to $48.5B (+$4.5B in Q1 alone); DSI acquisition adds $1.7B hyperscaler infrastructure exposure.

shifts.trend: Revenue YoY: +21% (FY25) → +26% (Q1 FY26); consistent organic execution
shifts.note: Absolute dollar growth increasing even as percent growth stays stable — revenue base compounding
$4.5B backlog add in a single quarter is the highest single-quarter backlog addition in company history

shifts.trend: DSI acquisition ($1.7B) adding direct hyperscaler infrastructure construction exposure; Electric segment growing fastest
shifts.note: M&A extending into hyperscaler-direct electrical construction — new adjacency to capture more DC build wallet

PWR.verdict: Quanta is a pure infrastructure demand beneficiary — backlog compounding at $4.5B/quarter provides 5+ years of revenue visibility. The DSI deal adds hyperscaler direct exposure. Execution capacity (labor, supply chain) is the key constraint.

SNOW.headline: Revenue growth consistent at 29–30%; NRR stable at 125% for two periods; $1M+ customer count growing +27% signals broad enterprise adoption.

shifts.trend: Product revenue YoY: +29% (Q3 FY26) → ~+30% (FY26 full year: $4.7B vs $3.6B)
shifts.note: Consistent 30% growth pace maintained despite consumption-model variability

shifts.trend: $1M+ customers: 576 (FY25) → 733 (FY26), +27%; total customers +21% to 13,328
shifts.note: Large-customer cohort growing faster than total customers — deepening enterprise penetration

shifts.trend: RPO: $7.9B (Q3) → $9.8B (FY26 year-end), +$1.9B sequentially; 46–48% expected in next 12 months
shifts.note: RPO growth steady — no acceleration or contraction

shifts.trend: NRR 125% stable across both periods; no degradation despite maturing install base
shifts.note: NRR stability is the key quality signal — expansion is sustaining at scale

SNOW.verdict: Consistent execution at 30% growth with stable 125% NRR and $1M+ customer expansion — no inflection but no deterioration. The AI/data workload thesis is intact; the question is whether Snowflake becomes the AI data layer or loses ground to warehouse-integrated alternatives.

SNPS.headline: Headline revenue growth +66% YoY on full Ansys inclusion; Design IP -6% while Design Automation +96%; China -22% ex-Ansys on BIS restrictions.

shifts.trend: FY25 +15% YoY (partial Ansys, $756.6M contribution) → Q1 FY26 +66% YoY (full Ansys, $885.6M Q1 contribution)
shifts.note: Ansys contribution growing each quarter — Synopsys EDA + Ansys simulation integration thesis beginning

shifts.trend: China revenue ex-Ansys: -22% (FY25) → still declining trend in Q1 FY26 per guidance language
shifts.note: BIS restrictions structurally reducing China addressable market; Design IP most exposed

shifts.trend: Design Automation +96% (Q1 FY26) vs Design IP -6% (Q1 FY26); Ansys (Simulation) added as third segment
shifts.note: Design Automation growing fastest (AI chip complexity) while Design IP dragged by China and competitive pressure — two-speed business

shifts.trend: Processor IP business sold to GlobalFoundries (announced Q1 FY26) — portfolio pruning, not expansion
shifts.note: Divesting non-core IP to focus on EDA + Simulation platform

SNPS.verdict: Ansys integration is the thesis driver — combined EDA + simulation is a unique platform with no direct competitor. China headwinds on Design IP are real and structural. Watch Ansys synergy revenue recognition and whether Design IP ex-China can stabilize.

VRT.headline: Revenue growth re-accelerated from +27.7% to +30%; gross margin expanded 400bps to 37.7%; Americas +53% driven by US data center build-out.

shifts.trend: Total revenue YoY: +27.7% (FY25) → +30% (Q1 FY26); Americas: +42% (FY25) → +53% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Americas growth re-acceleration is the strongest signal — US DC hyperscaler builds still expanding

shifts.trend: Gross margin: ~33–34% (FY25 implied) → 37.7% (Q1 FY26), +400bps
shifts.note: Pricing power and volume leverage driving simultaneous revenue and margin expansion

shifts.trend: Combined backlog $15.0B (FY25, doubled from $7.2B) → sustaining into Q1; $425–525M capex for capacity reaffirmed
shifts.note: Backlog doubling in FY25 now converting to revenue in FY26 — timing confirms the visibility

shifts.trend: FY26 capex $425–525M reaffirmed across both filings; no scale-back despite macro
shifts.note: Reaffirmation signals management confidence in sustained demand through end of CY2026

VRT.verdict: Vertiv is executing the cleanest growth + margin expansion combination in the power/cooling cohort — +30% revenue + 400bps margin expansion together are rare. Americas re-acceleration to +53% confirms US hyperscaler build-out is not decelerating.
